2	name: progress-critic
3	description: Fresh-context audit of recent iteration progress per active file/route. Detects helper-churn (each iter adds helpers but never converges), sorry-stall, repeated PARTIAL/INCOMPLETE patterns, route-going-in-circles, and *throughput drift* (this iter's progress is much slower than STRATEGY.md's estimate predicts). Also checks the planner's current PROGRESS.md proposal for dispatch-sanity issues (excessive objective count, files known to be blocked). Renders a CONVERGING / CHURNING / STUCK / UNCLEAR verdict per active route with specific corrective recommendations when CHURNING or STUCK.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [plan]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended every plan phase. When you do dispatch me,
11	    do so AFTER any strategy and blueprint reviewers in your catalog
12	    have returned, BEFORE deciding the iter's prover objectives. My
13	    verdict feeds directly into the planner's stuck-protocol gate.
14	
15	    **You may skip me this iter when ANY of:**
16	      - the prior iter ran no prover phase (e.g. a plan-only iter,
17	        or an escalation iter where the prover was intentionally
18	        skipped) — there is no new trajectory data to assess;
19	      - every active route's last K iters all carry the same
20	        signals as last iter (no new prover output, no new helpers
21	        added, no new blocker phrases) AND my prior verdict was
22	        CONVERGING with no must-fix-this-iter findings;
23	      - the only active route just completed in the prior iter (the
24	        sorry count went to zero and the route is closing out — there
25	        is no trajectory to extrapolate from).
26	
27	    Record the skip under `## Subagent skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`
28	    with a one-liner naming the condition met. Do NOT skip me on
29	    open routes with CHURNING or STUCK verdicts — the whole point of
30	    re-running me is to catch the planner walking into the same wall.
31	  - My value is fresh-context detection of "this iter looks like
32	    progress but the route has actually been churning for K iters."
33	    The plan agent, in the loop's context, is the worst-positioned
34	    judge of this — it ratifies its own recent decisions. I am the
35	    corrective.
36	
37	  ### Strict context discipline
38	
39	  Your directive must contain ONLY:
40	    - The active routes / files the planner is considering for this
41	      iter's prover assignment (one block per route).
42	    - For each, last K iters' SIGNALS extracted by you (the planner):
43	      sorry counts per iter, helpers added per iter, prover statuses
44	      (COMPLETE / PARTIAL / INCOMPLETE), recurring blocker phrases.
45	    - For each route, the strategy's CURRENT `Iters left` estimate
46	      and the iter at which the route entered its current phase —
47	      so the critic can compare "estimated K iters" against "elapsed
48	      K' iters". Lift these two values verbatim from the relevant
49	      `## Phases & estimations` row in STRATEGY.md; do NOT paste the
50	      whole strategy.
51	    - The planner's PROGRESS.md `## Current Objectives` proposal for
52	      this iter (file count + the basenames). Dispatch-sanity checks
53	      operate on this list — see "What you check" item 6.
54	    - K should be 3-5; more iters = better detection.
55	
56	  Your directive MUST NOT include:
57	    - STRATEGY.md (my question is convergence, not strategic
58	      soundness — that is the strategy critic's territory).
59	    - Blueprint chapters (math correctness is the blueprint
60	      reviewer's territory).
61	    - Iter sidecars' full content (just the extracted signals named
62	      above).
63	
64	  If the directive includes content I am NOT supposed to see, ignore
65	  it. My value depends on the narrow focus.
66	
67	  ### Acting on my verdicts
68	
69	  Verdicts are per-route:
70	
71	  - **CONVERGING** — the route is closing. Proceed with the next
72	    prover round.
73	  - **CHURNING** — each iter adds helpers but the residual hasn't
74	    shrunk. STOP assigning more helpers. My report names the
75	    corrective TYPE (blueprint expansion, Mathlib-idiom consult,
76	    structural refactor, route pivot); the planner picks the
77	    matching subagent from the catalog.
78	  - **STUCK** — no sorry-elimination or structural advance in K
79	    iters. STOP this route; address the blocker or pivot.
80	  - **UNCLEAR** — not enough signal yet (fresh route, 1-2 iters of
81	    data). Proceed but watch.
82	
83	  **CHURNING and STUCK are must-fix-this-iter.** The planner must
84	  respond — either with the action I recommend, or with an explicit

89	
90	# Progress Critic
91	
92	You are the fresh-context progress critic. The plan agent gives you per-route progress signals from the last K iters and asks one question: **is this route converging or just churning?**
93	
94	You don't read the strategy, the blueprint, or the project's mathematical content. Your value is the *signal* level — sorry counts, helper additions, recurring blocker phrases, prover status sequences. A route that adds 3 helpers per iter but whose residual stays the same is churning, regardless of whether the math is right.
95	
96	## Stance
97	
98	You are the corrective for a known failure pattern. The plan agent, embedded in the loop's context, naturally ratifies its own recent decisions: each iter it sees "we added helpers + sorry count dropped" and concludes "we're progressing." You see the longer arc: "5 iters, 14 helpers added, 1 sorry closed, residual identical to iter 1."
99	
100	The plan agent prefers CONVERGING verdicts because they let it continue. You should NOT give that bias the benefit of the doubt. When the signals point at churn, you say CHURNING. When the signals point at stall, you say STUCK. You don't soften.
101	
102	**The plan agent's laziness is also a signal.** The following planner behaviors are themselves churn patterns — they are not "good planning" and must not be treated as structural progress:
103	
104	- Dispatching a single prover when multiple files with complete blueprint chapters and open sorries are available and could be worked on at the same time. Under-loaded dispatch is artificial throttling and burns iterations.
105	- Constantly writing small edits on blueprints without closing any sorries.
106	- Reclassifying a route as "off-critical path" without a corresponding concrete plan and timeline for when it returns to the path. "Off-critical path" with no re-engagement plan is indefinite deferral.
107	- Keeping huge phases without decomposing them into sub-phases and therefore being reluctant to start on them because of their size. 
108	- Pivoting to a new route in a plan-only iter (no prover dispatch) and then pivoting again the following plan-only iter. Two consecutive plan-only pivots with no prover dispatch between them is the canonical avoidance pattern — the planner is iterating on the plan instead of testing it.
109	- Writing "we will address this next iter" or "this is deferred to future iterations" for the same item across ≥2 consecutive iter sidecars (when those are provided in signals). Deferral language persisting across iters without resolution is a STUCK signal.
110	
111	When you detect these behaviors in the signals, or any other signals that you believe being laziness and avoidance, include them in the relevant route's findings. A route that has suffered 3 consecutive "off-critical path" reclassifications or 3 consecutive single-prover dispatches when multiple files were ready is CHURNING by avoidance — the same as churn by helper accumulation.
112	
113	## Directive Format
114	
115	```markdown

146	- iter-NNN-K: COMPLETE | PARTIAL | INCOMPLETE — <one-line summary from prover report>

152	- ...
153	- iter-NNN-1: <N files dispatched>
154	
155	(Include this field when the planner extracted it. "N of M ready" format
156	is preferred — it exposes under-dispatch directly.)
157

159	- "<verbatim blocker phrase>" appears in iter-X, iter-Y, iter-Z reports — <one line>
160	- ...

168	- ...

171	- **`Iters left` from STRATEGY.md** (verbatim from the relevant `## Phases & estimations` row): <e.g. "3">
172	- **Elapsed iters in current phase**: <e.g. "9">

174

184	- **Files with complete blueprint chapters and open sorries (ready but not dispatched)**: <list or "none identified">

186

190	
191	## What you check
192	
193	For each route's block:
194	
195	1. **Sorry trajectory.** Is the count actually dropping over the K-iter window? "Down 1 in 5 iters" is stall; "down 1 every iter" is converging; "up and down by 1, net unchanged" is churn.
196	
197	2. **Helper accumulation vs payoff.** If helpers are being added but the residual doesn't shrink, that's churn. "We added 4 wrapper helpers this iter to set up next iter's closure" can be valid ONE time — when said 3 iters in a row, it's churn.
198	
199	3. **Recurring blockers.** A blocker phrase that appears in iter-X's prover report and then re-appears in iter-X+1 and iter-X+2 reports means the iterations are running into the same wall. That's STUCK.
200	
201	4. **Prover status pattern.** COMPLETE → COMPLETE → COMPLETE is converging. PARTIAL → PARTIAL → PARTIAL is churn. INCOMPLETE → INCOMPLETE is stuck. PARTIAL → INCOMPLETE → INCOMPLETE is regressing.
202	
203	5. **Under-dispatch pattern.** If the directive shows "N of M ready" with N < M for ≥2 consecutive iters, the planner is artificially throttling dispatch. This is churn by avoidance — not a prover problem but a planning problem. When N = 1 and M ≥ 3 for two or more consecutive iters, report as CHURNING with primary corrective "fill all ready lanes." Under-dispatch alone, without sorry-trajectory improvement, qualifies a route for CHURNING even if the prover is making local progress on the single dispatched file.
204	
205	6. **Avoidance patterns.** Check the `#### Route status changes per iter` and `#### Deferral language per iter` fields:
206	   - ≥2 consecutive iters where the route was listed as "off-critical path" or "deferred" without a re-engagement plan → CHURNING by avoidance.
207	   - ≥2 consecutive plan-only iters (prover count = 0 across the whole proposal, not just this route) where the route was nominally "active" → CHURNING (plan-phase-only meta-pattern clause).
208	   - Same deferral phrase ("will address next iter", "blocked pending upstream", "deferred to future work") appearing in ≥2 consecutive iter signals → STUCK by inaction.
209	   - Route pivoted AND the new route's primary blocker is inferably the same infrastructure gap as the old route's → CHURNING by rotation. (You do not have the blueprint or the strategy to verify this precisely; flag it as "possible rotation churn" and surface it as a CHALLENGE for the strategy-critic to confirm.)
210	
211	7. **PROGRESS.md dispatch sanity.** Independent of the route-level verdict, sanity-check the planner's current PROGRESS.md proposal:
212	
213	   - **Over the dispatch cap**: if file count > the cap shown in the directive (default 10), this is an automatic CHURNING-equivalent finding regardless of route convergence. Runaway fan-out (e.g. 27 provers in one iter) is the failure mode this check exists to prevent.
214	   - **Under-dispatch against ready files**: if the proposal lists fewer files than the "Files with complete blueprint chapters and open sorries (ready but not dispatched)" field shows are available, flag the gap explicitly. One or two fewer than ready is acceptable (planner may have strategic reasons); three or more fewer, or consistently fewer across iters, is an under-dispatch finding. Land this in must-fix-this-iter when the gap is ≥3 files or has persisted ≥2 iters.
215	   - **Bloat without route progress**: file count growing iter over iter while the route signals say CHURNING or STUCK suggests the planner is throwing more provers at the wall instead of escalating.
216	
217	   These are dispatch-level checks, not route-level. They land in a separate "PROGRESS.md dispatch sanity" block in your report.
218	
219	8. **Throughput honesty.** Compare `Iters left` (verbatim from STRATEGY.md's `## Phases & estimations`) against elapsed iters in the current phase. Bucket:
220	
221	   - **On schedule**: elapsed ≤ estimate.
222	   - **Slipping**: elapsed > estimate but ≤ 2× estimate.
223	   - **Over budget**: elapsed > 2× estimate.
224	   - **Estimate-free**: STRATEGY.md gives no number, or "?", for this row.
225	
226	   "Over budget" with a still-positive `Iters left` is the dishonest-estimate signature. "Estimate-free" with elapsed > 5 iters in a phase is also flag-worthy.
227	
228	## Verdict rules
229	
230	Apply these rules verbatim:
231	
232	- **CONVERGING**: sorry count strictly decreasing in K-iter window AND no recurring blocker AND no under-dispatch pattern AND no avoidance pattern AND planner's proposal looks like "finish what's started."
233	- **CHURNING**: any of the following:
234	  - helpers added in ≥2 of last K iters AND sorry count net unchanged or down by <1 per 2 iters AND no structural change in approach;
235	  - PARTIAL prover status ≥3 of last K iters;
236	  - **plan-phase-only meta-pattern**: ≥3 consecutive iters with zero prover dispatches on this route (no file ever appearing in `## Current Objectives`). Pure planning rounds without ever firing a prover is textbook stall.
237	  - **under-dispatch pattern**: prover count = 1 (or otherwise N < M ready) for ≥2 consecutive iters with no sorry-trajectory improvement attributable to the dispatch strategy (i.e. the filed sorry on the dispatched lane didn't close either).
238	  - **avoidance pattern**: ≥2 consecutive iters with "off-critical path" / "deferred" route status AND no re-engagement plan in the proposal.
239	- **STUCK**: sorry count unchanged across K iters AND prover statuses include INCOMPLETE OR recurring blocker phrase across ≥3 iters. OR: helpers added without any sorry-elimination across K iters. OR: same deferral phrase persisting across ≥2 consecutive iters.
240	- **UNCLEAR**: route is fresh (< K iters of data) OR signals are ambiguous.
241	
242	If multiple rules match a route, pick the worse verdict (CHURNING > CONVERGING; STUCK > CHURNING).
243	
244	## Recommended actions per verdict
245	
246	For CHURNING or STUCK, your report names ONE primary corrective TYPE. The planner consults the catalog for the matching subagent.
247	
248	- **Blueprint expansion** — the chapter's proof sketch is likely under-specified; the planner should expand it before more prover work.
249	- **Mathlib analogy consult** — the project may be using a parallel API or wrong predicate; the planner should consult Mathlib-idiom analysis on the route's load-bearing definitions.
250	- **Refactor** — the definition or file structure may be wrong; dispatch a structural subagent before more prover work.
251	- **Route pivot** — the strategic route may be wrong entirely; revise STRATEGY.md and pick a different route, then re-run any strategy critic mid-iter to validate.
252	- **Fill all ready lanes** — the planner is under-dispatching; all files with complete blueprint chapters and open sorries should be in `## Current Objectives` this iter, up to the dispatch cap.
253	- **Address deferred infrastructure** — the route has been marked off-critical-path or deferred; the planner must either write the blueprint chapter and open a prover lane this iter, or explicitly close the route as out-of-scope (which requires updating `## Goal` in STRATEGY.md if the goal depends on it).
254	- **User escalation** — none of the above will work; pause and request user input. Use sparingly — only when no automated corrective will resolve the stall.
255	
256	Pick ONE primary corrective per CHURNING/STUCK route. Multiple are allowed when truly necessary, listed in priority order.
257	
258	## Report format
259	
260	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/progress-critic-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
261	
262	**Omit-empty rule.** Only `## Slug`, `## Iteration`, `## Routes audited`, and `## Overall verdict` are required. Omit any section whose right answer is "nothing to report." Per-route blocks: when a route's verdict is CONVERGING with no recurring blockers, no avoidance patterns, and no secondary correctives, the block may be the trajectory + status pattern + verdict line only. Same for dispatch sanity: if dispatch is OK and no under-dispatch finding exists, render as a single line (`Verdict: OK — file count <N> within cap <C>, no under-dispatch`).
263	
264	```markdown
265	# Progress Critic Report
266	
267	## Slug
268	<slug>
269	
270	## Iteration
271	<NNN>
272	
273	## Routes audited
274	
275	For each route in the directive, one block:
276	
277	### Route: <name>
278	
279	- **Sorry trajectory**: <description, e.g. "5 → 5 → 4 → 4 → 4 across iter-100 to 104">
280	- **Helper accumulation**: <description, e.g. "13 helpers added across last 4 iters; 1 sorry closed">
281	- **Prover dispatch pattern**: <e.g. "1 of 3 ready files dispatched for 3 consecutive iters">
282	- **Recurring blockers**: <list or "none">
283	- **Avoidance patterns**: <list or "none" — name each: off-critical-path reclassification, consecutive plan-only iters, persistent deferral language, possible rotation churn>
284	- **Prover status pattern**: <e.g. "PARTIAL, PARTIAL, PARTIAL, PARTIAL">
285	- **Throughput**: ON_SCHEDULE | SLIPPING | OVER_BUDGET | ESTIMATE_FREE — <estimated K iters, elapsed K'>
286	- **Verdict**: CONVERGING | CHURNING | STUCK | UNCLEAR
287	- **Primary corrective** (if CHURNING/STUCK): <one of the actions above, with one paragraph of why>
288	- **Secondary correctives** (if applicable): <list>
289	
290	## PROGRESS.md dispatch sanity
291	
292	Independent of any route verdict.
293	
294	- **File count**: <N> (cap: <C>)
295	- **Ready but not dispatched**: <list of files with complete chapters and open sorries that are NOT in the proposal, or "none identified">
296	- **Over the cap**: yes | no
297	- **Under-dispatch finding**: yes | no — <if yes: gap size, how many consecutive iters>
298	- **Iter-over-iter trend**: <e.g. "1 → 1 → 1; consistently under-dispatching 3 ready files">
299	- **Verdict**: OK | OVER_CAP | UNDER_DISPATCH | BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS
300	  - OK: dispatch list is within cap, not under-dispatching ready files, and not growing while routes churn.
301	  - OVER_CAP: more files than the cap allows.
302	  - UNDER_DISPATCH: ≥3 ready files absent from the proposal, or consistently fewer than ready across ≥2 iters. Land in must-fix-this-iter.
303	  - BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS: file count growing iter over iter while route signals say CHURNING/STUCK.
304	
305	## Must-fix-this-iter <!-- omit entire section if no CHURNING/STUCK verdicts AND no OVER_BUDGET/OVER_CAP/UNDER_DISPATCH/BLOAT findings -->
306	
307	Every CHURNING and every STUCK verdict lands here automatically, every OVER_BUDGET throughput finding (with `Iters left > 0`), and every OVER_CAP / UNDER_DISPATCH / BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS dispatch verdict. Do not under-classify.
308	
309	- Route <name>: <verdict> — primary corrective: <action>. Why: <one line>.
310	- Route <name>: OVER_BUDGET — STRATEGY.md estimates <K> iters, elapsed <K'>. Revise the estimate or escalate.
311	- Route <name>: avoidance pattern — <specific pattern detected>. Primary corrective: <action>.
312	- Dispatch: UNDER_DISPATCH — <N> ready files absent from proposal for <M> consecutive iters. Fill all ready lanes this iter.
313	- Dispatch: OVER_CAP — planner listed <N> files (cap <C>). Re-prioritize; defer <N-C> files.
314	- Dispatch: BLOAT_WITHOUT_PROGRESS — file count <a> → <b> → <c> while routes <X>, <Y> remain CHURNING.
315

44	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
45	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
46	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
47	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
48	with the alternating-face differential.

50	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
51	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
52	   above direct sums.
53	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.

56	Key API:
57	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
58	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
59	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
60	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
61	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
62	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
63	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
64	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
65	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
66	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
67	
68	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
69	path above.
70	
71	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
73	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
75	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
76	
77	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
78	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
79	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
80	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
81	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
82	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
83	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
84	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
85	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
86	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
87	
88	Key API:
89	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
90	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
91	
92	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
93	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.

96	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
97	
98	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
99	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
100	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
101	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
102	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
103	
104	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
105	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
106	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
107	
108	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
109	
110	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
111	
112	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
113	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
114	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
115	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
116	
117	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
118	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
119	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
120	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
121	
122	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
123	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
124	
125	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
126	
127	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
128	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
129	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
130	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
131	
132	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
133	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
134	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
135	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
136	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
137	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
138	
139	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
140	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
141	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
142	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
143	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
144	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
145	  subst e; simp
146	
147	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
148	
149	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
150	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
151	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
152	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
153	
154	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
155	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
156	
157	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
158	Mathlib counterpart. -/
159	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
160	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
161	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
162	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
163	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
164	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
165	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
166	  map_id n := by
167	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
168	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
169	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
170	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
171	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
172	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
173	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
174	      eqToHom_map]
175	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
176	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
177	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
178	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
179	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
180	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
181	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
182	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
183	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
184	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
185	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
186	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
187	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
188	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
189	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
190	    congr 2
191	
192	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
193	
194	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
195	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
196	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
197	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
198	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
199	
200	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
201	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
202	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
203	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
204	
205	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
206	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
207	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
208	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
209	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
210	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
211	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
212	  rfl

214	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation and cover structure presheaf -/
215	
216	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
217	
218	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
219	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
220	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
221	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
222	
223	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
224	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
225	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
226	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
227	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
228	
229	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
230	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
231	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
232	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
233	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
234	
235	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
236	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
237	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
238	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
239	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
240	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
241	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
242	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
243	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
244	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
245	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
246	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
247	    congr 1
248	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
249	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
250	  rw [hmap] at hnat
251	  rw [hnat]
252	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
253	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
254	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
255	
256	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
257	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
258	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
259	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
260	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
261	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
262	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
263	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
264	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
265	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
266	
267	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
268	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
269	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
270	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
271	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
272	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
273	
274	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
275	
276	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
277	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
278	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
279	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
280	
281	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
282	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
283	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
284	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
285	
286	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
287	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
288	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
289	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/
290	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (i : Fin 2) :
291	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
292	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
293	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
294	  intro σ
295	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicial, cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
296	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
297	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 _ σ)
298	
299	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
300	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
301	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
302	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
303	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
304	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
305	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0
306	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0 :=
307	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
308	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
309	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicial.obj` types — matching
310	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
311	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)
312	  -- equal and block the rewrites.
313	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
314	      ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
315	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
316	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
317	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug]
318	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
319	    abel
320	  rw [hd]; exact main
321	
322	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf
323	`O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` vanishes. Obtained from `cechFree_d_comp_aug` by cancelling the
324	mono `image.ι`. This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
325	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`cechFreeComplexAug`). -/
326	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
327	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) = 0 := by
328	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
329	    Limits.zero_comp]
330	  exact cechFree_d_comp_aug 𝒰

332	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
333	
334	The chain map from the free Čech complex to the cover structure presheaf concentrated in degree
335	`0`, whose degree-`0` component is the canonical map `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` onto the
336	image presheaf. The chain-map condition `d ≫ aug = 0` is `cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage`. The
337	quasi-isomorphism claim `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` asserts this map is a quasi-isomorphism.
338	
339	Project-local: the augmented free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
340	noncomputable def cechFreeComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
341	    cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰 ⟶
342	      (ChainComplex.single₀ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) :=
343	  ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).toSingle₀Equiv (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)).symm
344	    ⟨Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰), cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage 𝒰⟩
345	
346	/-- The degree-`0` component of the augmentation chain map is the canonical map onto the image
347	presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
348	lemma cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
349	    (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).f 0 = Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) := by
350	  rw [cechFreeComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]

352	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms
353	
354	Homology in `PresheafOfModules R` is computed objectwise: the evaluation functors
355	`PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V` are jointly conservative and preserve homology, so a
356	morphism of complexes of presheaves of modules is a quasi-isomorphism as soon as each of its
357	evaluations is.  These three lemmas package that reduction.  Mathlib has the single-functor
358	statement `HomologicalComplex.quasiIso_map_iff_of_preservesHomology` (which needs the functor to
359	reflect isomorphisms — false for a single evaluation), but not the joint-conservativity version
360	needed to reduce a quasi-isomorphism of presheaf-of-module complexes to its sectionwise checks. -/
361	
362	open HomologicalComplex in
363	/-- For a functor `F` preserving homology, if the induced map on the homology of the mapped
364	complexes is an isomorphism, then `F` applied to the homology map is an isomorphism.  This is the
365	naturality square of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso` read off at a fixed degree. -/
366	private lemma isIso_Fmap_homologyMap {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {C₁ C₂ : Type*}
367	    [Category C₁] [Category C₂] [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive C₂]
368	    {K L : HomologicalComplex C₁ c} (φ : K ⟶ L) (F : C₁ ⥤ C₂) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
369	    (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
370	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).HasHomology i]
371	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj L).HasHomology i]
372	    (hiso : IsIso (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i)) :
373	    IsIso (F.map (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ i)) := by
374	  have key := ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso_hom_naturality ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ) F
375	  haveI hmid : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
376	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := hiso
377	  change IsIso (F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)))
378	  haveI hcomp : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
379	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)) ≫
380	      (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj L).mapHomologyIso F).hom) := inferInstance
381	  haveI hcomp2 : IsIso ((((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom ≫

386	/-- **Joint conservativity of evaluations.** A morphism of presheaves of `R`-modules is an
387	isomorphism as soon as each of its evaluations `(evaluation R V).map g` is, since the underlying
388	presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of presheaves is an
389	isomorphism iff it is so objectwise.  Project-local: the joint-conservativity packaging is not in
390	Mathlib. -/
391	private lemma isIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat)
392	    {M N : PresheafOfModules R} (g : M ⟶ N)
393	    (h : ∀ V, IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).map g)) : IsIso g := by
394	  suffices hh : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g) from
395	    isIso_of_reflects_iso g (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)
396	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
397	  intro V
398	  have hV := h V
399	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map] at hV
400	  haveI : IsIso (g.app V) := hV
401	  have e : ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g).app V
402	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab).map (g.app V) := rfl
403	  rw [e]
404	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab) (g.app V)

406	/-- **Objectwise reduction of quasi-isomorphisms.** A morphism `φ` of chain complexes of
407	presheaves of `R`-modules is a quasi-isomorphism if, for every object `V`, its evaluation
408	`(evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex |>.map φ` (a morphism of complexes of `R(V)`-modules) is a
409	quasi-isomorphism.  This is the formal core of the sectionwise argument that the free Čech complex

412	Project-local: combines `isIso_of_evaluation` (joint conservativity) with `isIso_Fmap_homologyMap`

416	    [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
417	    (h : ∀ V, QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ)) :
418	    QuasiIso φ := by
419	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
420	  intro i
421	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
422	  apply isIso_of_evaluation R
423	  intro V
424	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map]
425	  haveI hqV : QuasiIsoAt (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i :=
426	    (h V).quasiIsoAt i

428	  exact isIso_Fmap_homologyMap φ (PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V) i hqV

430	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — combinatorial contracting-homotopy engine
431	
432	The objectwise reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` turns `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` into a
433	per-open-`V` statement: the evaluation of the augmented free Čech complex at `V` is the
434	augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with
435	constant coefficients the ring `O_X(V)`.  This section ports the constant-coefficient
436	combinatorial contracting homotopy — the prepend-`i_fix` map and its `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id`
437	identity — into this file as a self-contained algebraic engine.
438	
439	This is the free-side analogue of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` in `CechAcyclic.lean`
440	(those declarations are `private` there, hence unavailable here); the proofs are the same
441	alternating-sum cancellation, specialised to the constant coefficient module.  Here `M` is
442	the constant coefficient module (to be `O_X(V)` in the application) and `ι` the index type
443	(to be `I₁(V)`). -/
444	
445	namespace FreeCechEngine
446	
447	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
448	
449	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
450	`(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`, the alternating sum of the index-dropping
451	maps.  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential`. -/
452	def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
453	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
454	
455	/-- The contracting homotopy: prepend the fixed index `r`.  `(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`.
456	Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`. -/
457	def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
458	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
459	
460	@[simp] lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
461	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
462	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
463	
464	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(k+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the `k`-th
465	coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
466	lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
467	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
468	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
469	  funext l
470	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
471	  · simp
472	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
473	
474	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on the constant-coefficient
475	Čech complex (Stacks `lemma-homology-complex`, the `dh + hd = id` computation).  Free-side
476	port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec`. -/
477	lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
478	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
479	  funext σ
480	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
481	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
482	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
483	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
484	    funext i; simp
485	  rw [h0]
486	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
487	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
488	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
489	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
490	
491	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then `t = d (h t)`. -/
492	lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
493	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
494	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
495	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
496	  exact h
497	
498	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
499	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
500	lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
501	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
502	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
503	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
504	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
505	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
506	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
507	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
508	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
509	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
510	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
511	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
512	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
513	
514	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex, via the
515	sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)`. -/
516	lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
517	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
518	  funext σ
519	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
520	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
521	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
522	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
523	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
524	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
525	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
526	      funext k
527	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
528	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
529	    simp only [harg]
530	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
531	    simp
532	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
533	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
534	    intro hj
535	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
536	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
537	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
538	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
539	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
540	
541	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex
542	(`Function.Exact` form).  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_exact`. -/
543	lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
544	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
545	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
546	  intro x
547	  constructor
548	  · intro hx
549	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
550	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
551	    exact combDifferential_comp y
552	
553	end FreeCechEngine

555	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sectionwise reduction of the free Čech complex -/
556	
557	/-- **Evaluation preserves the degreewise coproduct of the free Čech complex.**
558	
559	The objectwise-reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` requires understanding the evaluation of
560	`cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰` at an open `V`.  Since the degree-`p` term is the coproduct
561	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and the evaluation functor
562	preserves finite colimits (`PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits`,
563	with `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` finite as `𝒰.I₀` is finite), evaluating commutes with the
564	coproduct:
565	`(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p) ≅ ∐_σ (evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`.
566	This is the degree-`p` entry point for the sectionwise description
567	(blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`); each evaluated summand
568	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
569	`O_X(V)`-module on the (subsingleton) hom-set `V ⟶ W`, i.e. `O_X(V)` if `V ≤ W` and `0`
570	otherwise.
571	
572	Project-local: the assembly of evaluation-preserves-coproduct with the free Čech complex
573	has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
574	noncomputable def cechFreeEval_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
575	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (p : ℕ) :
576	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)
577	      ≅ ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
578	          (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj
579	            (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
580	  haveI : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
581	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V) := by
582	    haveI := PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits X.ringCatSheaf.obj V
583	    infer_instance
584	  exact Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso _ _
585	
586	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ⊄ W` gives the zero module.**
587	
588	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
589	`O_X(V)`-module on the hom-set `V ⟶ W`.  When `V ≰ W` that hom-set is empty, so the module
590	is `0`.  This is the per-summand input to the empty case of the sectionwise reduction (and
591	kills the non-`I₁` summands in the nonempty case).
592	
593	Project-local: a degreewise vanishing statement for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
594	lemma freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : ¬ V ≤ W) :
595	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
596	      (freeYoneda.obj W)) := by
597	  haveI : IsEmpty (V ⟶ W) := ⟨fun f => h (leOfHom f)⟩
598	  haveI : Subsingleton ↑((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
599	      (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)) :=
600	    ⟨fun a b => Finsupp.ext (fun x => (IsEmpty.false x).elim)⟩
601	  exact ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
602	
603	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ≤ W` gives `O_X(V)`.**
604	
605	When `V ≤ W` the hom-set `V ⟶ W` is a singleton (`Unique`), so the free `O_X(V)`-module on it
606	is `O_X(V)` itself.  This is the per-summand identification of the surviving (`I₁`) summands in
607	the sectionwise reduction (blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`): together with
608	`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` it gives the description
609	`K(𝒰)_p(V) = ⊕_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} O_X(V)`.
610	
611	Project-local: a degreewise identification for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
612	noncomputable def freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ W) :
613	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)
614	      ≅ ModuleCat.of (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
615	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
616	  haveI : Unique (V ⟶ W) := ⟨⟨homOfLE h⟩, fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
617	  (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique _ _ (V ⟶ W)).toModuleIso

619	/-- A coproduct of zero objects is a zero object: every coproduct injection out of a zero
620	object is zero, so the identity of the coproduct is zero. -/
621	lemma isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]
622	    {β : Type*} (f : β → C) [Limits.HasCoproduct f]
623	    (h : ∀ b, Limits.IsZero (f b)) : Limits.IsZero (∐ f) := by
624	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
625	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
626	  intro b
627	  rw [Category.comp_id, Limits.comp_zero]
628	  exact (h b).eq_zero_of_src _
629	
630	/-- **Empty case of the sectionwise reduction (object level).**
631	
632	If no cover member `U_i` contains `V` (i.e. `I₁(V) = ∅`), then the degree-`p` term of the
633	free Čech complex evaluates to the zero module at `V`.  Indeed every multi-index intersection
634	`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ 0)`, so `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` would force `V` into a
635	cover member, contradiction; hence each summand vanishes by `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`
636	and the coproduct is zero.  This is the object-level input to
637	`cech_free_eval_empty`. -/
638	lemma cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
639	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
640	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
641	      ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)) := by
642	  refine Limits.IsZero.of_iso ?_ (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p)
643	  apply isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero
644	  intro σ
645	  apply freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le
646	  intro hle
647	  exact hV (σ 0) (le_trans hle (iInf_le (fun k => coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)) 0))

650	
651	If no cover member contains `V` (`I₁(V) = ∅`), then `O_𝒰(V) = 0`.  The cover structure
652	presheaf is the image of the augmentation `cechFreeAug`, so `O_𝒰` is an epimorphic image of

654	epimorphism out of the zero module `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_0)`

656	lemma coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
657	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
658	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj

660	  change Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
661	      (Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))
662	  haveI : Epi ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
663	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) :=
664	    Functor.map_epi (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))

666	  exact Limits.IsZero.of_epi
667	    ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
668	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))
669	    (cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V 0 hV)

671	/-- The homology of a homological complex vanishes in any degree where the complex object

673	lemma isZero_homology_of_isZero_X {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]

676	  rw [← K.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology i, HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,

690	      (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).map
691	        (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)) := by
692	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
693	  intro i
694	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
695	  refine Limits.isIso_of_source_target_iso_zero _
696	    (Limits.IsZero.isoZero ?_) (Limits.IsZero.isoZero ?_)
697	  · exact isZero_homology_of_isZero_X _ i (cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V i hV)

992	-/

11	/-!
12	# Čech bridge — assembly layer
13	
14	This file is the downstream assembly layer that consumes both:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex` (from `PresheafCech.lean`): the section Čech
17	  cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F) : CochainComplex Ab ℕ` built cosimplicially from
18	  `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.
19	
20	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex` (from `FreePresheafComplex.lean`): the
21	  free-presheaf chain complex `K(𝒰)_• : ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose
22	  degree-`p` term is `∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
23	
24	The reason this file must sit downstream of both is that `FreePresheafComplex.lean`
25	already imports `PresheafCech.lean`, so any file needing both must import
26	`FreePresheafComplex.lean` (which transitively brings in `PresheafCech.lean`).
27	
28	## Declarations
29	
30	- `cechComplex_hom_identification` (**proved**, line ~241): the per-degree `Ab`-isomorphism
31	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` intertwining the differentials, assembled cosimplicially
32	  via `homCechSectionCosimplicialIso` and `(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso`.
33	
34	- `homCechComplexMapOpIso` / `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib
35	  supplement below): the contravariant-transport bridge. `homCechComplexMapOpIso` identifies
36	  the alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` with the mapped opposite
37	  `Hom(-, F)((K(𝒰)_•)ᵒᵖ)` of the free Čech complex (same degreewise terms; the differential
38	  squares are the degreewise identity `homCechComplex_d_eq`).  `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso`
39	  composes it with `cechComplex_hom_identification` to identify `Č•(coverOpen 𝒰, F)` with the
40	  mapped opposite of the free complex.  This is the precise bridge that lets
41	  `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` turn Lane-1's free resolution into Čech
42	  acyclicity in a single step.
43	
44	- `preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective` /
45	  `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (**proved**, project-local Mathlib
46	  supplement below): the categorical bridge step. For an injective object `I` of an abelian
47	  category, the contravariant `Hom(-, I) = preadditiveYoneda.obj I` is exact (preserves
48	  finite colimits, hence homology), so it carries a quasi-isomorphism of chain complexes to
49	  a quasi-isomorphism of the `Hom`-cochain complex. This is the ingredient that turns the
50	  free-complex resolution `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` (Lane 1) and
51	  `cechComplex_hom_identification` into the positive-degree vanishing below.
52	
53	- `injective_cech_acyclic` (planned, gated on Lane-1 `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`): Čech
54	  cohomology vanishes in positive degrees for injective sheaves — now a one-step assembly of
55	  `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` with `sectionCechComplexMapOpIso` once Lane 1
56	  lands.
57	
58	- `ses_cech_h1` (planned): the short exact sequence in Čech H¹ induced by a short exact
59	  sequence of sheaves.
60	
61	- `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` / `affine_serre_vanishing` (planned): the comparison
62	  isomorphism `Ȟ•(𝒰, F) ≅ H•(X, F)` on affine schemes / for acyclic covers, leading to
63	  Serre's vanishing theorem.
64	-/

66	universe u
67	
68	open CategoryTheory Limits

70	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

72	/-
73	Planner strategy for `cechComplex_hom_identification`:
74	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	Goal: construct a cochain-complex isomorphism
77	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`
78	where:
79	  • `K(𝒰)_p = ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
80	    (the degree-`p` term of `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`)
81	  • `Č^p(𝒰, F) = ∏_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
82	    (the degree-`p` term of `sectionCechComplex 𝒰 F`)
83	  Both complexes live in `Ab` after applying the global-sections functor.
84	
85	Step 1 — per-degree hom-coproduct duality:
86	  For each `p`, the Yoneda/free-presheaf adjunction gives
87	    `Hom(freeYoneda.obj V, F) ≅ F.obj V`  (natural in `V`).
88	  This is exactly `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` from `PresheafCech.lean`.
89	  Combined with the coproduct-hom identity
90	    `Hom(∐_σ A_σ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(A_σ, F)`
91	  (via `preadditiveYoneda` preservation of limits, or hand-rolled from
92	  `Limits.Sigma.desc` / `Limits.Sigma.ι`), we get a degree-wise `Ab`-iso
93	    `Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) ≅ ∏_σ F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
94	
95	Step 2 — differential intertwining:
96	  Show that the degree-wise isos commute with the differentials.  The differential on
97	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)` is precomposition with the Čech boundary `d_p : K(𝒰)_{p+1} → K(𝒰)_p`
98	  (the alternating sum of face maps of `cechFreeSimplicial`).  The differential on
99	  `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the alternating sum of restriction maps built by `sectionCechComplex`.
100	  These match under the per-degree iso by naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
101	
102	Step 3 — assemble:
103	  Use `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents` (or equivalent) to combine the per-degree
104	  isos into a full cochain-complex iso, supplying the naturality squares from Step 2.
105	
106	Key API:
107	  • `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` (PresheafCech.lean): the per-degree iso.
108	  • `Limits.Sigma.desc`, `Limits.Sigma.ι`: coproduct universal property in
109	    `X.PresheafOfModules`.
110	  • `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`: assemble component-wise isos into a
111	    full complex iso.
112	  • `sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean): the target complex.
113	  • `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreePresheafComplex_X` (FreePresheafComplex.lean):
114	    the source complex and its degreewise unfolding lemma.
115	-/
116	
117	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech hom-identification -/
118	
119	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
120	
121	/-- **The hom cosimplicial abelian group `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`.**
122	
123	The cosimplicial object in `Ab` obtained by applying the contravariant additive functor
124	`Hom_{PMod}(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` to the free Čech simplicial object
125	`cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Its value in degree `p` is the hom-group
126	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`, and its alternating-coface-map cochain complex is the left-hand
127	side of the Čech hom-identification.  Defining it this way makes the differential of the
128	hom-complex literally the alternating sum of `Hom(faceᵢ, F)`, so the identification with
129	`sectionCechComplex` reduces to a cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
130	
131	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `cechFreeSimplicial` nor this contravariant transport. -/
132	noncomputable def homCechCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
133	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} :=
134	  (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).rightOp ⋙ preadditiveYoneda.obj F
135	
136	/-- **The hom cochain complex `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`** — the left-hand side of the Čech
137	hom-identification.
138	
139	The alternating-coface-map cochain complex of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F`. Built the same way as
140	`sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean) so that the planned identification
141	`cechComplex_hom_identification : homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F` is just
142	`(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso` of the cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
143	
144	Project-local: the hom-complex of the free Čech resolution has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
145	noncomputable def homCechComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
146	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
147	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F)
148	
149	/-- **Per-degree component of the Čech hom-identification.**
150	
151	For each simplicial degree `n`, the abelian-group isomorphism
152	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_n, F) ≅ ∏_σ F(⨅ₖ U(σ k))` assembled from (i) the coproduct–hom duality
153	`Hom(∐_σ Aσ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(Aσ, F)` (the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` preserves the limit,
154	so `piComparison` is an iso, combined with `opCoproductIsoProduct`), and (ii) the per-index
155	free–Yoneda section identification `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
156	
157	Project-local: this is the degreewise core of `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`. -/
158	noncomputable def homCechSectionIsoApp (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
159	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) :
160	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).obj n ≅ (sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F).obj n :=
161	  (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapIso
162	      (opCoproductIsoProduct
163	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))
164	    ≪≫ asIso (piComparison (preadditiveYoneda.obj F)
165	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => Opposite.op (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))))
166	    ≪≫ Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
167	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso)
168	
169	/-- `Pi.mapIso e` is by definition `Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom)`. -/
170	private lemma pi_mapIso_hom_eq {β : Type*} {C : Type*} [Category C] {f g : β → C}
171	    [HasProductsOfShape β C] (e : ∀ b, f b ≅ g b) :
172	    (Limits.Pi.mapIso e).hom = Limits.Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom) := rfl
173	
174	/-- Characterizing property of `homCechSectionIsoApp`: its `σ`-component is precomposition
175	with the coproduct injection `Sigma.ι σ` followed by the free–Yoneda section identification.
176	Reduces the naturality of the assembled iso to the naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`. -/
177	private lemma homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
178	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) (σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
179	    (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F n).hom ≫
180	        Pi.π (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
181	          F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))) σ =
182	      (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map
183	          (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
184	            freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) σ).op ≫
185	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom := by
186	  rw [homCechSectionIsoApp, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
187	    pi_mapIso_hom_eq]
188	  simp only [Category.assoc]
189	  erw [Pi.map_π, piComparison_comp_π_assoc]
190	  rw [← Category.assoc]
191	  congr 1
192	  exact ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
193	    (congrArg _ (opCoproductIsoProduct_hom_comp_π σ))
194	
195	/-- **Naturality of the free–Yoneda section identification in the open.**
196	
197	For an inclusion of opens `h : V ⟶ W`, precomposition with `freeYoneda.map h` on the hom-side
198	corresponds to restriction `F.presheaf.map h.op` on the section side.  This is the single
199	naturality square that powers the cosimplicial naturality of the Čech hom-identification. -/
200	private lemma freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality {V W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X}
201	    (h : V ⟶ W) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
202	    (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map (freeYoneda.map h).op ≫
203	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv V F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom
204	      = (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv W F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom ≫ F.presheaf.map h.op := by
205	  ext ψ
206	  show freeYonedaHomEquiv V F (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
207	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op)) (freeYonedaHomEquiv W F ψ)
208	  show yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ))
209	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op))
210	          (yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ))
211	  rw [show PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
212	        = yoneda.map h ≫ PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ from
213	      (PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv_naturality_left (yoneda.map h) ψ,
214	    ← yonedaEquiv_naturality]
215	  rfl
216	
217	/-- **The cosimplicial natural isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.**
218	
219	Assembles the per-degree isomorphisms `homCechSectionIsoApp` into a natural isomorphism of
220	cosimplicial abelian groups. The naturality square reduces, via `homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π`
221	in both degrees, to the single naturality square `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality` of the
222	free–Yoneda section identification.
223	
224	Project-local: the cosimplicial comparison of the hom-complex with the section complex has no
225	Mathlib counterpart. -/
226	noncomputable def homCechSectionCosimplicialIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
227	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
228	    homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
229	  NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F) (by
230	    intro n m f
231	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
232	    intro σ
233	    dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]
234	    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π]
235	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F m σ]
236	    conv_rhs => rw [← Category.assoc]
237	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F n (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f))]
238	    erw [Category.assoc]
239	    erw [← freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality
240	        (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f) σ)) F]
241	    dsimp only [homCechCosimplicial, Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map]
242	    erw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
243	    congr 1
244	    congr 1
245	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
246	    simp only [unop_comp]
247	    dsimp only [cechFreeSimplicial]
248	    erw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
249	    rfl
250	    )
251	
252	/-- **The Čech hom-identification** (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`).
253	
254	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` identifying the
255	hom-complex of the free Čech resolution with the section Čech complex of `F`. Obtained by
256	applying the alternating-coface-map complex functor to the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
257	`homCechSectionCosimplicialIso`, so the differential intertwining is automatic from the
258	cosimplicial naturality.
259	
260	Project-local: the comparison of the free-resolution hom-complex with the section complex has
261	no Mathlib counterpart. -/
262	noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identification (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
263	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
264	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
265	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso 𝒰 F)
266	
267	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — hom-complex as contravariant transport
268	
269	The injective-acyclicity assembly (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, gated on Lane-1's
270	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`) needs `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` — the alternating *coface* complex of
271	the hom-cosimplicial object `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` — to be identified with the
272	contravariant transport `Hom(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` of the *opposite* of the free
273	Čech chain complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Once this identification is in hand,
274	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (applied to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op`) and
275	`cechComplex_hom_identification` combine in a single step to turn the free resolution into
276	Čech acyclicity of injective sheaves.
277	
278	Project-local: the comparison of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped opposite
279	of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
280	
281	/-- The `i`-th coface of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` is `Hom(-, F)` applied to the opposite of
282	the `i`-th face of `cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Holds definitionally (both unfold to
283	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).map (SimplexCategory.δ i).op).op`);
284	isolated as a `rfl` lemma to drive the differential identification `homCechComplex_d_eq`. -/
285	private lemma homCechCosimplicial_δ (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
286	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {n : ℕ} (i : Fin (n + 2)) :
287	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).δ i
288	      = (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i).op :=
289	  rfl
290	
291	/-- **Degreewise differential identification** of the hom-complex with the mapped opposite of
292	the free Čech complex.  Both differentials are alternating sums of (co)faces; pushing the
293	opposite and the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` through the alternating sum on the right and
294	using `homCechCosimplicial_δ` matches them term by term.  This is the naturality input for the
295	cochain-complex isomorphism `homCechComplexMapOpIso`. -/
296	private lemma homCechComplex_d_eq (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
297	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
298	    (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
299	      = (((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
300	          (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))).d p (p + 1) := by
301	  have hL : (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
302	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F) p :=
303	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
304	  have hR : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d (p + 1) p
305	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) p :=
306	    ChainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
307	  rw [hL, AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
308	    Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, HomologicalComplex.op_d, hR,
309	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD]
310	  have hop := CategoryTheory.op_sum
311	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk (p + 1))))
312	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
313	    (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (p + 2)))
314	    (fun i => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i)
315	  erw [hop, Functor.map_sum]
316	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
317	  rw [homCechCosimplicial_δ, CategoryTheory.op_zsmul]
318	  erw [Functor.map_zsmul]
319	  rfl
320	
321	/-- **Hom-complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
322	
323	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Hom(-, F)((K(𝒰)_•)ᵒᵖ)` identifying the
324	alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` with the contravariant transport
325	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex` of the opposite of the free Čech chain
326	complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Degreewise the two complexes have the *same* term
327	(`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`), so the components are identities; the differential squares are
328	`homCechComplex_d_eq`.
329	
330	This is the bridge that turns Lane-1's free resolution into Čech acyclicity: applying
331	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op` produces a
332	quasi-isomorphism of mapped-opposite complexes, and this iso transports it onto
333	`homCechComplex 𝒰 F` (hence, via `cechComplex_hom_identification`, onto `sectionCechComplex`).
334	
335	Project-local: the identification of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped
336	opposite of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
337	noncomputable def homCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
338	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
339	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅
340	      ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
341	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰)) :=
342	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
343	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
344	    exact (Category.id_comp _).trans
345	      ((homCechComplex_d_eq 𝒰 F i).symm.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm))
346	
347	/-- **Section Čech complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
348
